bootstrap-rules.purpose: Seed durable engineering documentation and preset-specific process workflows into a project when Compass is first used.

trigger.rule: When one or more seed docs are missing from the target project's docs directory, offer orientation presets before running bootstrap.
Do not run bootstrap immediately with a silent default preset.
Bootstrap may run without asking only when Compass selects existing-architecture-lock from observed existing-project evidence.
Do not run bootstrap when the user explicitly asks for no file changes.
Do not overwrite existing files unless the user explicitly asks for force mode.
After bootstrap, adapt copied preset docs to the target language, framework, and project conventions before reporting docs ready.

command.path-resolution: Resolve scripts/bootstrap-docs.sh relative to the loaded Compass SKILL.md directory, not relative to the target workspace.

orientation-lock.purpose: Lock architecture, foundation principles, and development workflow before implementation begins.
orientation-lock.selection-rule: Ask the user to choose a preset before bootstrap unless existing-architecture-lock is selected from observed existing-project evidence.

preset.architecture: Clean Architecture plus explicit folder/file structure
preset.short: Enterprise-grade Clean Architecture layering, explicit folder/file structure, SOLID, and TDD.
preset.best-fit: Frontend, backend, full-stack, API, and business systems that need protected dependency direction, clear boundaries, and test-first discipline.

preset.architecture: Vertical Slice Architecture plus explicit feature-slice structure
preset.short: Vertical Slice Architecture, explicit feature-slice structure, CUPID/YAGNI/KISS, and incremental tests.
preset.best-fit: Small to medium products that benefit from feature-local change, low ceremony, and avoiding over-layering.

preset.architecture: Domain-Driven Design plus explicit bounded-context structure
preset.short: Domain-Driven Design, explicit bounded-context structure, domain modeling, SOLID, and BDD plus TDD.
preset.best-fit: Complex business domains that need ubiquitous language, bounded contexts, aggregates, and executable behavior examples.

preset.short: Preserve and document the architecture, principles, and workflow already present in the project.
preset.best-fit: Mature projects with a coherent existing structure that should be extended instead of replaced.

preset.short: Research current sources, compare options, cite evidence, then lock the selected direction.
preset.best-fit: Unknown domains, unfamiliar teams, or explicit requests to research architecture alternatives.
preset.rule: Browse current sources, compare at least three options, prefer primary or authoritative sources, and cite links before finalizing the lock.

language-adaptation.purpose: Prevent preset reference docs from being copied as language-specific boilerplate into projects using a different stack.
language-adaptation.rule: Detect the target language and framework from existing files when possible.
If the project is empty or ambiguous, ask the developer which language or stack to target before adapting docs.
Use preset docs as detailed references, not immutable text.
Rewrite generated folder structures, file names, entrypoints, adapter names, test placement, and examples to target-stack idioms.
Preserve architecture principles, dependency direction, SOLID rules, TDD gates, checklists, and completion evidence.
Do not create static stack profiles in the Compass skill; the LLM performs the adaptation at bootstrap time.
For clean-solid-tdd, do not leave Go-only examples such as cmd, internal, .go files, module.go, or adapters/postgres in non-Go projects unless the target project actually uses those conventions.

For clean-solid-tdd, review and adapt docs/architecture/folder-file-structure.md, docs/architecture/clean-architecture.md, docs/process/tdd-workflow.md, and docs/process/workflows.xml when stack-specific wording appears.

completion-evidence.item: Report which copied docs were adapted from preset references.
Report whether docs/process/workflows.xml came from the selected preset.
Report whether force mode was used.
